item.title: Body found in Tampa Bay identified as 2nd missing doctoral student
item.description: Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon were last seen​ in the Tampa area on April 16. Limon's roommate has been charged with their murders.

item.title: Appeals court blocks mailing of abortion pill mifepristone in U.S.
item.description: A federal appeals court has restricted access to one of the most common means of abortion in the U.S. by blocking the mailing of mifepristone.

item.title: Investigators believe stolen vape pen caused Washington state high school stabbings
item.description: After multiple stabbings on Thursday at a high school in Tacoma, Washington, a student is facing multiple assault charges. Five people were wounded, including the suspect, and investigators believe it all started over a stolen vape pen.

item.title: Spirit Airlines poised to cease operations as soon as Saturday, barring last minute intervention
item.description: Spirit Airlines is essentially out of available cash, so barring last-minute intervention, the company is poised to cease operations as soon as Saturday morning, potentially stranding thousands of fliers. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Fierce winds, pounding rain sweep through Texas
item.description: Fourteen million Americans across the South are under threat as severe weather stretches from New Mexico to Louisiana. Some of the worst of it hit Texas, parts of which have seen deadly flooding, torrential rain and hurricane-force winds. Rob Marciano is tracking it all.

item.title: What would a Spirit Airlines shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights if the budget airline ceases operations this weekend.

item.title: Perfume Capital of the World | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: In Grasse, France, flowers for the world's most famous perfume have been grown and harvested for more than a century. 60 Minutes visits the town that became the perfume capital of the world. Sunday.

item.title: Ford CEO says company is focusing on making vehicles more affordable
item.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley tells CBS News, "Most of our new models are going to be more affordable versions."

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Japan attacks Pearl Harbor in 1941
item.description: In the 20th century, no event rocked America more than the surprise Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces a very particular part of vital CBS News Radio coverage from that day.

item.title: Trump "not satisfied" with new peace deal offered by Iran
item.description: Iran said it had offered a new proposal to the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Pentagon plans to remove 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, officials say
item.description: The Pentagon is planning to withdraw about 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, senior officials said on Friday. CBS News intelligence and national security reporter Olivia Gazis has more details.

item.title: Battery company working to reduce data center impact on U.S. power grid
item.description: Powerful artificial intelligence data centers are putting a significant strain on the nation's power grid, but one U.S.-based company has a proposal to help solve the issue. Jon Parella, CEO and founder of Terraflow Energy, joins to discuss.

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down as soon as Saturday
item.description: Negotiations over a $500 million dollar government aid package for Spirit stalled after bondholders balked at the terms.

item.title: 18-year-old facing federal charges in deadly Kentucky bank robbery
item.description: Kentucky State Police said a man went to a U.S. Bank in Berea, Kentucky, and shot and killed a man and a woman, both employees at the bank.

item.title: Pickleball Inc. $225 million deal creates new home for sport
item.description: The deal merged Major League Pickleball and the Carvana PPA Tour, two of the nascent sport's most active entities under one company.

item.title: "Game of chicken" between U.S., Iran to continue for now, national security analyst says
item.description: Iran has sent the U.S. its revised proposal to end the war. The latest offer came as a top Iranian commander said any U.S. attack would draw a "painful retaliation." President Trump addressed the negotiations earlier on Friday. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Biden makes first endorsement since leaving White House
item.description: Former President Joe Biden returned to the political arena on Friday, giving his first endorsement since leaving office. NOTUS politics and policy reporter Oriana Gonzalez and House Leadership reporter for Politico Riley Rogerson join to discuss.

item.title: "Spirit is operating as usual," Spirit says on reports of shutdown plans
item.description: Spirit Airlines is planning to end operations as soon as Saturday, unless the government intervenes at the last minute. Axios business reporter Nathan Bomey joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: More Americans are quitting their U.S. jobs to work abroad, study finds
item.description: Since 2021, the share of U.S.-based employees who have left their jobs to work in another country has more than doubled.

item.title: Could the FCC really yank ABC's TV licenses amid Trump spat with Kimmel?
item.description: Federal telecom regulators can revoke broadcast licenses, but legal experts say the FCC would face a tough road in forcing ABC to go dark.

item.title: Louisiana Rep. Troy Carter calls Supreme Court map decision a "gut punch" to democracy
item.description: Louisiana is suspending its May 16 House primaries following the Supreme Court's decision to strike down its congressional map. The high court found the state relied too heavily on race when it created a second majority-Black district. Democratic Rep. Troy Carter of Louisiana joins "The Takeout" to discuss the ruling.

item.title: Analyzing newly-released videos of suspect in correspondents' dinner attack
item.description: U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro released new videos on Thursday night of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect before and during the attack. CBS News' Anna Schecter and Samantha Vinograd join to break down the footage.

item.title: Judge rebukes prosecutors in hearing for accused correspondents' dinner gunman
item.description: A federal judge scolded prosecutors for pushing to move forward with detention proceedings for accused correspondents' dinner gunman Cole Allen, even though Allen agreed to remain in custody.

item.title: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated" as authorization deadline arrives
item.description: President Trump told Congress in a letter on Friday that "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated." Friday marks the 60-day deadline for the president to carry out military actions without congressional approval. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean joins to discuss.

item.title: Why Spirit is on the brink of collapse, and what it could mean for other airlines
item.description: CBS News has learned Spirit Airlines could go out of business as soon as Saturday after bailout talks with the Trump White House stalled. CBS News senior transportation correspondent Kris Van Cleave has the details. Then, Politico aviation reporter Oriana Pawlyk joins to assess the potential economic fallout.

item.title: Birds of War | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: Colombia is home to about 2,000 bird species, more than anywhere else on Earth. Decades of conflict kept people out of many areas, preserving the birds' habitat. Now birders are flocking in. Anderson Cooper reports, Sunday.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: Louisiana suspends House primaries for May 16 elections
item.description: Louisiana Secretary of State Nancy Landry said Thursday that the state will suspend its May 16 House primaries in the wake of the Supreme Court striking down​ the state's Congressional map.

item.title: What states could try to redistrict and add more GOP seats
item.description: The Supreme Court's ruling on Wednesday about Louisiana's congressional map could have implications for several states as it narrowed the section of the Voting Rights Act about majority-minority districts.
